steps.title: Identify the key inference signal
steps.content: The passage says Davis "had to listen repeatedly to the vinyl recordings, which weren't very clear" in order to transcribe the improvised lyrics. This labor-intensive process only makes sense if reliable transcriptions did not already exist — otherwise Davis could have used them instead.

steps.title: Test each choice against that inference
steps.content:
• "The songs have grown in popularity since Rainey and Smith first sang them.": The passage says nothing about the songs' popularity over time. No evidence supports this. ✗
  • "There were more recordings made of Rainey's songs than there were of Smith's.": The passage makes no comparison between the number of recordings of Rainey versus Smith. ✗
  • "There were few, if any, reliable transcriptions of Smith's and Rainey's improvised lyrics when Davis began her research.": Davis's painstaking effort to transcribe from unclear recordings directly implies that reliable transcriptions were not available to her. If they had been, she would not have needed to do this work. ✓
  • "According to Davis, the songs sung by Rainey were more musically innovative than those sung by Smith typically were.": The passage treats both artists similarly in terms of improvisation and wordplay, and makes no claim about one being more innovative than the other. ✗
That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
